title: hymenophyllum gorgoneum
intro: Hymenophyllum gorgoneum, also known as the maidenhair fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Hymenophyllum gorgoneum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused oil, although modern herbal preparations may also include it as a dried extract or capsule.
